vBRIEFInfo.description: HOT adoption blocker: scripts/preflight_branch.py prints a U+2713 check mark on its success path. On Windows where Python's stdout defaults to cp1252, this raises UnicodeEncodeError after the gate has already approved the commit, blocking every commit on a default Windows install. Fix: force UTF-8 stdout reconfigure at script entry; audit every hook-invoked deft script for the same shape; warn at task setup time when cp1252 is detected.

plan.title: scripts/preflight_branch.py crashes with UnicodeEncodeError on Windows (cp1252) -- blocks every commit

narratives.Problem: scripts/preflight_branch.py (the pre-commit branch-protection hook installed by `task setup`, #747) prints `\u2713` on its success path. On Windows, Python's stdout default is cp1252 (or cp437 depending on console host), neither of which has a glyph for U+2713. `codecs.charmap_encode` raises UnicodeEncodeError; the hook exits non-zero with a traceback; git aborts the commit. Crucially: the gate has ALREADY APPROVED the commit before the print fails, so the user sees a Python traceback after a successful check, which is maximally confusing. Workaround (`PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 git commit ...`) is undiscoverable. Reproduction captured during the #810 fixup chore commit on this Windows session.
narratives.Overview: Three coordinated changes per the issue. Change 1: force UTF-8 stdout/stderr reconfigure at the top of scripts/preflight_branch.py main() with errors='replace' as a belt-and-suspenders fallback. Change 2: audit every scripts/*.py referenced from .githooks/ and apply the same fix; pin via a NEW tests/cli/test_hooks_encoding.py contract test that wraps stdout in a cp1252 BytesIO TextIOWrapper and verifies non-ASCII print succeeds. Change 3: task setup detects cp1252 stdout at install time and warns the user with the actionable PYTHONIOENCODING fix (informational on current versions because the hooks self-reconfigure).
narratives.Background: Python TextIOWrapper.reconfigure was added in 3.7 and is available on stdout/stderr when those streams ARE TextIOWrapper instances (terminal attach). When stdout is captured by git as a PIPE, reconfigure is a no-op or unavailable -- guarded by `hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure")`. errors='replace' means rare encoding-failure environments see printable replacement characters instead of an unhandled traceback. The gate logic itself (#747) is correct and should not change; only the print path does.
narratives.Constraint: MUST NOT regress the gate logic (#747). MUST NOT change the success glyph from `\u2713` to ASCII `OK` -- the glyph is intentional and matches framework CLI conventions per the issue Out-of-Scope. MUST guard `reconfigure` calls behind hasattr (PIPE-mode safety). MUST audit every hook-invoked script under scripts/ -- not only preflight_branch.py. MUST NOT auto-set PYTHONIOENCODING from task setup (mutating the user's environment in a way that surprises other tooling is the wrong shape). SHOULD also surface the same warning from the agent-side environment-preflight (skills/deft-directive-setup/SKILL.md) if applicable.
narratives.Risk: Very low. The reconfigure block is idempotent and well-known; guarded by hasattr. Worst case (PIPE-mode + no reconfigure + downstream consumer of stdout) is unchanged from today. Audit may surface other scripts but the pattern is uniform.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) scripts/preflight_branch.py reconfigures stdout/stderr to UTF-8 with errors='replace' at main() entry. (2) The success-path U+2713 glyph prints cleanly on Windows cp1252 (verified by contract test). (3) Audit identifies every hook-invoked script under scripts/ and applies the same fix. (4) NEW tests/cli/test_hooks_encoding.py asserts non-ASCII print succeeds for each hook script in a cp1252-emulated stdout. (5) task setup detects cp1252 at install time and emits the actionable warning. (6) CHANGELOG [Unreleased] / Fixed entry. (7) meta/lessons.md one-liner on Windows + cp1252 + Python stdout default as a recurring adoption-blocker class.
narratives.Test: tests/cli/test_hooks_encoding.py (NEW) -- for each hook script: monkeypatch sys.stdout to a TextIOWrapper wrapping a BytesIO with encoding='cp1252' and errors='strict'; invoke main() (or the relevant entry point); assert no UnicodeEncodeError; assert sys.stdout.encoding == 'utf-8' after reconfigure; assert the U+2713 glyph reaches the underlying buffer encoded as UTF-8 bytes 0xE2 0x9C 0x93. Skip on platforms where reconfigure is unavailable (Python < 3.7 -- not in support matrix; defense in depth).
narratives.Action: 1. Edit scripts/preflight_branch.py main() to add the stdout/stderr reconfigure block at entry (before any print). 2. Audit scripts/ for every Python file referenced from .githooks/ -- apply the same fix to each. 3. Add tests/cli/test_hooks_encoding.py with the contract test described above. 4. Add the cp1252 detection + warning to tasks/setup.yml or the equivalent setup script. 5. CHANGELOG [Unreleased] / Fixed entry citing #814. 6. meta/lessons.md one-liner on the Windows + cp1252 + Python stdout default pattern. 7. task check (with PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 as a workaround during the build itself, until the fix lands and is self-applying); commit; push; PR.
narratives.Urgency: HOT adoption blocker. Every fresh Windows install of `task setup` produces a clone that fails on its first commit. The user encounters a Python traceback with no in-band guidance. Should land FIRST in the Phase 1 cohort because every other Phase 1 fix that ships from a Windows machine without PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 set is itself blocked by this bug. Recurrence record: #810 fixup chore commit on the 2026-05-01 Windows session.

items.title: Force UTF-8 stdout/stderr reconfigure at the top of scripts/preflight_branch.py main()

narrative.Action: Insert verbatim per the issue body's 'Change 1' block: guarded `sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8', errors='replace')` (and stderr) before any print. Place before the gate logic so even error-path prints are encoding-safe.

items.title: Audit + fix every scripts/*.py referenced from .githooks/

narrative.Action: Greppable surface: any script under scripts/ referenced from .githooks/. Currently includes scripts/preflight_branch.py (this issue). Apply the same reconfigure block to each. Future-proof: add a content test asserting any new hook script carries the reconfigure block (optional; recommended).

For each hook script: monkeypatch sys.stdout to a TextIOWrapper wrapping a BytesIO with encoding='cp1252' errors='strict'; invoke main() (or relevant entry); assert non-ASCII glyph print succeeds and stdout encoding is utf-8 post-reconfigure.

items.title: Add Windows cp1252 detection + warning to task setup

narrative.Action: task setup detects the Windows-cp1252 environment at install time and emits the actionable warning per the issue body's 'Change 3' block. Informational only on current versions because hooks self-reconfigure.

items.title: Add meta/lessons.md one-liner on Windows + cp1252 pattern

narrative.Action: ## Windows cp1252 stdout default breaks non-ASCII prints (2026-05) -- short cross-reference to this issue + the self-reconfigure pattern. Per [AXIOM] strongest-applicable-layer, the rule body lives in the script + tests, not duplicated in prose.

fix(scripts/preflight_branch.py + audit): UTF-8 self-reconfigure at hook-script entry; add task setup cp1252 warning; add tests/cli/test_hooks_encoding.py contract test; closes a Windows adoption blocker that broke every commit on default cp1252 stdout (#814).

items.title: Run task check and confirm clean

narrative.Action: Pre-PR gate. May require PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 in the agent's environment for the build itself if running on Windows before the self-reconfigure lands.

references.title: Issue #814: scripts/preflight_branch.py crashes with UnicodeEncodeError on Windows (cp1252) when printing the success glyph -- blocks every commit
Issue #747: detection-bound branch gate -- the surface this affects
Issue #810: implementation-intent gate -- the session that surfaced this; the chore commit was blocked until PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 was applied
Issue #768: cmd_agents_refresh -- another deft-managed surface that prints non-ASCII; audit Change 2 should include it

x-migrator.PhaseDescription: Urgent fixes to land ahead of broader roadmap work -- adoption blockers and bugs that block consumer adoption or successful first-session UX.

x-tracking.estimated_size: M (1 fix + audit + 1 new test file + task setup warning + lessons + CHANGELOG); priority HOT -- blocks every Windows commit

x-swarm-guidance.rationale: HOT bug; clear single-PR boundary; reference implementation in the issue body. Multiple files but uniform pattern. Agent should branch, fix preflight_branch.py + audit hook scripts, add the contract test, add the task setup warning + lessons entry + CHANGELOG, run task check, commit, push, open PR. Agent should self-apply PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 in its build environment until the fix is committed if running on Windows.
x-swarm-guidance.pr_title_suggestion: fix(scripts/preflight_branch.py): UTF-8 self-reconfigure at hook entry to unblock Windows cp1252 commits (#814)
